---
title: "2. Descriptive Analysis"
---

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "../src")
import utils

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import seaborn as sns

utils.set_theme()

dfs = utils.load_all()
baseline  = dfs["baseline"]
schools   = dfs["schools"]
math_chat = dfs["math_chat"]
math_quiz = dfs["math_quiz"]
voc_chat  = dfs["voc_chat"]
voc_steps = dfs["voc_steps"]

# Merge treatment arm into baseline
treatment_lookup = schools[["school_id", "treatment", "treatment_label"]].copy()
baseline = baseline.merge(treatment_lookup, left_on="school_code", right_on="school_id", how="left")
```

## 2.1 Sample Characteristics (Table 1)

Mean (SD) for key baseline variables, overall and by treatment arm.

```{python}
def describe_var(df, col, label, treatment_col="treatment_label"):
    """Return mean (SD) or proportion by treatment arm and overall."""
    rows = {}
    for arm in ["Overall"] + list(utils.TREATMENT_MAP.values()):
        subset = df if arm == "Overall" else df[df[treatment_col] == arm]
        data = subset[col].dropna()
        if data.dtype == object or set(data.unique()) <= {0, 1, 0.0, 1.0}:
            rows[arm] = f"{data.mean()*100:.1f}%"
        else:
            rows[arm] = f"{data.mean():.2f} ({data.std():.2f})"
    return {"Variable": label, **rows}

table1_rows = []
for section, variables in utils.BALANCE_VARS.items():
    table1_rows.append({"Variable": f"**{section}**", "Overall": "", **{v: "" for v in utils.TREATMENT_MAP.values()}})
    for col, label in variables.items():
        if col in baseline.columns:
            table1_rows.append(describe_var(baseline, col, f"  {label}"))

table1 = pd.DataFrame(table1_rows)
print(table1.to_string(index=False))
table1.to_csv("../outputs/tables/02_table1_characteristics.csv", index=False)
```

## 2.2 Psychological Scale Distributions

Distribution of key psychological scales by treatment arm.

```{python}
scale_labels = {
    "grit_avg": "Grit",
    "mindset_avg": "Growth mindset",
    "locus_avg": "Locus of control",
    "efficacy_avg": "Self-efficacy",
    "math_enjoy_avg": "Math enjoyment",
    "math_anxiety_avg": "Math anxiety",
    "math_selfconcept_avg": "Math self-concept",
    "belonging_avg": "School belonging",
}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 4, figsize=(14, 6), sharey=False)
axes = axes.flatten()

colors = list(utils.TREATMENT_COLORS.values())

for ax, (col, label) in zip(axes, scale_labels.items()):
    for i, (arm, color) in enumerate(utils.TREATMENT_COLORS.items()):
        subset = baseline[baseline["treatment_label"] == arm][col].dropna()
        ax.hist(subset, bins=15, alpha=0.55, color=color, label=arm, density=True)
    ax.set_title(label, fontsize=10)
    ax.set_xlabel("Scale (1–5)")
    ax.set_xlim(1, 5)

handles = [mpatches.Patch(color=c, label=l, alpha=0.7)
           for l, c in utils.TREATMENT_COLORS.items()]
fig.legend(handles=handles, loc="lower center", ncol=3, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.02))
fig.suptitle("Distribution of psychological scales by treatment arm", y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/02_scale_distributions.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

## 2.3 Baseline Math Score

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))

# Overall distribution
ax = axes[0]
ax.hist(baseline["score_pct_baseline"].dropna(), bins=20, color="#4C72B0", edgecolor="white")
ax.set_xlabel("Baseline math score (%)")
ax.set_title("Distribution — full sample")
ax.axvline(baseline["score_pct_baseline"].mean(), color="red", linestyle="--", label=f"Mean = {baseline['score_pct_baseline'].mean():.1f}%")
ax.legend()

# By treatment arm
ax = axes[1]
for arm, color in utils.TREATMENT_COLORS.items():
    subset = baseline[baseline["treatment_label"] == arm]["score_pct_baseline"].dropna()
    ax.hist(subset, bins=15, alpha=0.55, color=color, label=arm, density=True)
ax.set_xlabel("Baseline math score (%)")
ax.set_title("Distribution by treatment arm")
ax.legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/02_baseline_math_score.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()

print(baseline.groupby("treatment_label")["score_pct_baseline"].describe().round(2))
```

## 2.4 Platform Engagement — Math Module

```{python}
# Message counts per student, by location
student_msgs = (
    math_chat[math_chat["is_student"] == 1]
    .groupby(["user_id", "location"])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="n_messages")
)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))
for ax, loc in zip(axes, ["home", "school"]):
    data = student_msgs[student_msgs["location"] == loc]["n_messages"]
    ax.hist(data.clip(upper=data.quantile(0.99)), bins=30, color="#4C72B0", edgecolor="white")
    ax.set_title(f"Messages per student — {loc} (clipped at 99th pct)")
    ax.set_xlabel("Number of student messages")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/02_math_engagement.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()

print("Summary — student messages per user:")
print(student_msgs.groupby("location")["n_messages"].describe().round(1))
```

### Answer correctness by topic

```{python}
correct_by_topic = (
    math_chat[math_chat["is_correct"].notna()]
    .groupby("topic_name")["is_correct"]
    .agg(["mean", "count"])
    .rename(columns={"mean": "pct_correct", "count": "n"})
    .sort_values("pct_correct")
    .reset_index()
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, max(4, len(correct_by_topic) * 0.4)))
bars = ax.barh(correct_by_topic["topic_name"], correct_by_topic["pct_correct"] * 100, color="#4C72B0")
ax.set_xlabel("% Correct answers")
ax.set_title("Math performance by topic (school chat)")
ax.set_xlim(0, 100)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/02_correct_by_topic.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
```

## 2.5 Platform Engagement — Vocational Module

### Vocational journey step completion (funnel)

```{python}
step_cols = [f"step_{i}_has_data" for i in range(1, 9) if f"step_{i}_has_data" in voc_steps.columns]
step_labels = [f"Step {i}" for i in range(1, len(step_cols) + 1)]

completion_rates = [voc_steps[col].fillna(0).astype(bool).mean() * 100 for col in step_cols]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
ax.bar(step_labels, completion_rates, color="#55A868", edgecolor="white")
ax.set_ylabel("% of users who reached this step")
ax.set_title("Vocational journey — step completion funnel")
ax.set_ylim(0, 110)
for i, v in enumerate(completion_rates):
    ax.text(i, v + 1.5, f"{v:.1f}%", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/02_voc_funnel.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
```

### Time spent on the vocational journey

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
time_data = voc_steps["total_minutes_spent"].dropna()
ax.hist(time_data.clip(upper=time_data.quantile(0.99)), bins=30, color="#55A868", edgecolor="white")
ax.set_xlabel("Total minutes spent (clipped at 99th pct)")
ax.set_title("Distribution of time on vocational journey")
print(f"Median: {time_data.median():.1f} min | Mean: {time_data.mean():.1f} min | Max: {time_data.max():.1f} min")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/02_voc_time.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
```

## 2.6 Post-secondary Aspirations

```{python}
aspiration_cols = {
    "plan_after_school": "Plans after school",
    "knows_career": "Has identified a career (%)",
    "plans_higher_ed": "Plans for higher education (%)",
    "enrolled_academy": "Currently enrolled in academy (%)",
}

for col, label in aspiration_cols.items():
    if col in baseline.columns:
        if baseline[col].nunique() <= 6:
            print(f"\n{label}:")
            print(baseline[col].value_counts(normalize=True).mul(100).round(1).to_string())
```
